vulnerability.reference: Mathematicians defer to multi-agent consensus as a proxy for proof validity, bypassing peer review.
vulnerability.generated: Institutional blind trust in formal verification without semantic review, rendering researchers unable to detect semantically misaligned proofs.

impact.reference: Contamination of mathematical literature with unfounded results, undermining future research.
impact.generated: Corruption of formal mathematics literature with unfounded theorems, eroding trust and misdirecting subsequent research.

dose_response.reference: Multi-agent collaboration amplifies propagation of formally-valid but semantically-misaligned proofs, which when relied upon by mathematicians for proof discovery leads to contamination of mathematical literature and collapse of trust.
dose_response.generated: Multi-agent amplification of formally-valid but semantically-misaligned proofs, through reliance by mathematicians on multi-agent LLM provers, triggers blind institutional trust in formal verification without semantic review, corrupting formal mathematics with unfounded theorems, eroding trust and misdirecting research.
